constraints: Reuse the existing data-theme token-sheet pattern in src/ui/src/index.css rather than introducing a parallel theming mechanism
Persist the palette choice in localStorage alongside the existing warren.theme key, with safe fallbacks on invalid/missing values
Preserve FOUC prevention: apply the palette synchronously before React paints, matching the existing index.html bootstrap
Stay within warren UI conventions (PascalCase components, Tailwind tokens) and pass bun run check:all
intent.goal: Let warren UI users select a named color palette (accent theme) that layers on top of the existing light/dark/system mode and persists across reloads.
non_goals: Replacing or removing the existing light/dark/system mode toggle
Per-user server-side theme storage (selection stays client-side like the current theme)
Custom user-defined palettes or a color picker — only a fixed set of named palettes
success_criteria: A user can pick from at least two named palettes and the accent colors update live without reload
The selected palette survives a page reload and a light/dark mode switch
An invalid or absent stored palette value falls back to the default palette without errors
